justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified (generates new trace ID, severing chain). NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific caller details. Consequence explicitly named — on-call engineer must manually correlate across services, estimating 30-60 minute triage vs 30 seconds. Cross-service SLO gap identified (no end-to-end order lifecycle metric). Specific fix recommendations provided for each trace break (extract from Kafka headers, propagate X-Trace-ID on all HTTP calls). Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough trace break analysis — identified all 4 broken/risk boundaries with precise descriptions of why each is broken
Concrete consequence narrative: 3am on-call scenario with step-by-step manual correlation process and time estimate
Cross-service SLO gap called out explicitly with specific examples ('what percentage of orders are fulfilled within X minutes?')
Fix recommendations are specific and actionable per service, including deployment ordering advice (consumer before producer)
Goes beyond minimum rubric — identifies Kafka consumer lag metric gap, circuit breaker absence, timeout/retry gaps, and span hierarchy missing
Correctly routes escalations to appropriate team members with clear rationale
